title: [lichen_l2.c:996] late init failure leaves RX callback partially registered
description: Function: lichen_l2_iface_init. Error handling: If lichen_log_link_local_from_eui64 fails (line 995-998), the code jumps to fail_late_init which clears the RX callback (line 1028). However, link_ctx_initialized was already set to 1 (line 991) and is cleared in the failure path (line 1031). The sequence is: link_ctx initialized, RX callback set, link_ctx_initialized=1, then failure, then callback cleared and link_ctx_initialized=0. This is correct. But between lines 978-991, if an RX arrives, it would see link_ctx_initialized=0 (not yet set) and drop the frame. This is the intended behavior per comments. Priority 3 - verified correct.
close_reason: Fixed: Moved atomic_set(&iface_init_failed, 1) to BEFORE lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(NULL, NULL) in fail_late_init. This ensures any RX callback invoked between registration and cleanup sees the failure flag and bails out immediately, preventing races where a callback could proceed into lichen_l2_input() while cleanup is in progress.
